labs/common/lab2_agent_solution.txt.title: get_weather() request URL
note: **Builds the Open-Meteo forecast URL for today's high, low, and conditions.**
- Free weather API, no key required
- get_weather becomes a tool the agent can call

labs/common/lab2_agent_solution.txt.title: Fetch and parse forecast
note: **Calls the weather API and returns high, low, and a friendly conditions string.**
- WEATHER_CODES maps the numeric code to text
- raise_for_status() surfaces HTTP errors to the retry logic

labs/common/lab2_agent_solution.txt.title: convert_c_to_f() tool
note: **Second tool: converts Celsius to Fahrenheit, returned as a dict.**

labs/common/lab2_agent_solution.txt.title: Tool registry
note: **Maps tool names to functions so the agent can dispatch by name.**
- The LLM emits a name; TOOLS.get() looks up the function

labs/common/lab2_agent_solution.txt.title: Local LLM client
note: **Connects to the local Ollama llama3.2 model; temperature 0 keeps replies deterministic.**

labs/common/lab2_agent_solution.txt.anchor: """Execute the TAO loop, letting the AI decide which tools to call."""
labs/common/lab2_agent_solution.txt.title: Seed the TAO loop
note: **Starts the Thought-Action-Observation loop with the system prompt and user question.**
- messages grows each turn as the agent reasons and observes

labs/common/lab2_agent_solution.txt.title: Get the model's next step
note: **Asks the LLM for its next Thought/Action, then prints it.**

labs/common/lab2_agent_solution.txt.title: Parse Action and Args
note: **Pulls the tool name and JSON args out of the LLM's text response.**
- Brittle string parsing — later labs switch to regex

labs/common/lab2_agent_solution.txt.title: Look up the tool
note: **Resolves the parsed name to an actual function via the TOOLS registry.**

labs/common/lab2_agent_solution.txt.title: Run the selected tool
note: **Parses JSON args, calls the tool, and records that it ran.**
- tools_called tracks coverage so the loop knows when it's done

labs/common/lab2_agent_solution.txt.title: Feed observation back
note: **Appends the model's reply and the tool result so the next turn sees them.**
- Closing this loop is what makes the agent 'agentic'

labs/common/lab2_agent_solution.txt.anchor: You are a weather agent with two tools:
labs/common/lab2_agent_solution.txt.endAnchor: 3. Give your Final answer with the temperature in Fahrenheit
labs/common/lab2_agent_solution.txt.title: System prompt: role & tools
note: **Defines the agent's persona, its two tools, and the order it must call them.**
- The LLM only knows about tools listed here
- WORKFLOW forces get_weather then convert_c_to_f before the answer

labs/common/lab2_agent_solution.txt.anchor: For each tool call, output EXACTLY:
labs/common/lab2_agent_solution.txt.endAnchor: Final: Today in London will be Partly cloudy with a high of 65.3°F.
labs/common/lab2_agent_solution.txt.title: System prompt: TAO format
note: **Pins the exact Thought/Action/Args wording the response parser depends on.**
- The worked example teaches the pattern by demonstration

labs/common/lab2_agent_solution.txt.endAnchor: 8. NEVER output a Final answer until you have received Observations from both tools
labs/common/lab2_agent_solution.txt.title: System prompt: guardrails
note: **Hard constraints that keep the agent honest and on-format.**
- No self-done math, no guessed temps, one step per turn

labs/common/lab3_server_solution.txt.anchor: def get_weather(lat: float, lon: float) -> dict:
labs/common/lab3_server_solution.txt.title: get_weather MCP tool
note: **Declares the weather tool the MCP server exposes to clients.**
- The @mcp.tool decorator above registers it automatically

labs/common/lab3_server_solution.txt.title: Weather request URL
note: **Builds the Open-Meteo current-weather URL from the lat/lon args.**

labs/common/lab3_server_solution.txt.title: Parse the weather response
note: **Pulls temperature and weathercode from the JSON into a tidy dict.**
- WEATHER_CODES turns the numeric code into readable conditions

labs/common/lab3_server_solution.txt.title: convert_c_to_f MCP tool
note: **A second server tool: plain Celsius-to-Fahrenheit math.**

labs/common/lab3_server_solution.txt.title: Geocoding request setup
note: **Targets the Open-Meteo geocoding API to turn a place name into coordinates.**
- last_error holds the reason if every attempt fails

labs/common/lab3_server_solution.txt.title: Start the MCP server
note: **Runs the server over HTTP on port 8000 at /mcp/ so clients can connect.**

labs/common/lab3_agent_solution_dynamic..title: Format MCP tool schemas
note: **Turns the server's live tool list into a text catalogue for the system prompt.**
- Runs after list_tools(), so the prompt reflects real tools, not hard-coded ones

labs/common/lab3_agent_solution_dynamic..anchor: async def run_dynamic(city: str, max_steps: int = 10) -> None:
labs/common/lab3_agent_solution_dynamic..title: run_dynamic() entry point
note: **Defines the dynamic loop where the LLM, not the code, picks which tools to call.**
- max_steps caps iterations to prevent infinite loops

labs/common/lab3_agent_solution_dynamic..anchor: # ── Discover available tools from the MCP server ───────────
labs/common/lab3_agent_solution_dynamic..title: Discover tools at runtime
note: **Asks the MCP server what tools it has and injects them into the system prompt.**
- The core MCP idea: no hard-coded tool list on the client

labs/common/lab3_agent_solution_dynamic..title: System prompt with live tools
note: **Seeds the conversation with the dynamically built tool descriptions.**

labs/common/lab3_agent_solution_dynamic..title: Banner and context store
note: **Prints the discovered tools and sets up a dict to collect results across steps.**

labs/common/lab3_agent_solution_dynamic..title: Get and parse the decision
note: **Asks the LLM for its next Action and bails if it can't be parsed.**
- ACTION_RE regex replaces Lab 2's brittle string splitting

labs/common/lab3_agent_solution_dynamic..anchor: # Dynamically call the tool the LLM selected
labs/common/lab3_agent_solution_dynamic..title: Announce the tool call
note: **Logs which MCP tool the LLM chose and the args it passed.**

labs/common/lab3_agent_solution_dynamic..title: Call the MCP tool
note: **Dispatches the LLM-selected tool to the server and unwraps the result.**
- Nothing here is tool-specific — any discovered tool works

labs/common/lab3_agent_solution_dynamic..title: Record the observation
note: **Stores the result and feeds it back as an Observation for the next turn.**
- Closes the TAO loop so the agent can reason on what it learned

labs/common/lab3_agent_solution_dynamic..endAnchor: Args: <valid JSON arguments for the tool>
labs/common/lab3_agent_solution_dynamic..title: System prompt: format + live tools
note: **Template whose {mcp_tool_descriptions} slot is filled with tools discovered at runtime.**
- Sets the Thought/Action/Args format and the DONE signal

labs/common/lab3_agent_solution_dynamic..endAnchor: Do NOT add extra text. Do NOT explain after your three lines.
labs/common/lab3_agent_solution_dynamic..title: System prompt: worked examples
note: **Few-shot examples teaching the exact call pattern for each tool.**
- Doubled braces {{}} survive .format() as literal JSON braces

labs/common/lab5_agent_solution.txt.anchor: # Regex for parsing LLM responses (same pattern as Labs 2 and 3)
labs/common/lab5_agent_solution.txt.title: Response-parsing regexes
note: **Compiled patterns that pull Action and Args out of the LLM's text.**
- Same approach reused from Labs 2 and 3

labs/common/lab5_agent_solution.txt.anchor: # Initialize the embedding model and database connection once at startup
labs/common/lab5_agent_solution.txt.title: Embedding function
note: **Loads the embedding model used to vectorize search queries.**
- Must match the model that indexed the PDFs in Lab 4

labs/common/lab5_agent_solution.txt.title: RAG retrieval tool
note: **Opens the Lab 4 vector DB and defines search_offices — the 'Retrieval' in RAG.**
- Embeds the query and returns the top matching office chunks
- This is a LOCAL tool, unlike the remote MCP ones

labs/common/lab5_agent_solution.txt.title: Format MCP tool schemas
note: **Builds a text catalogue of the server's tools for the system prompt.**
- Same dynamic-discovery helper introduced in Lab 3

labs/common/lab5_agent_solution.txt.title: Local LLM client
note: **Creates the Ollama model that drives the agentic RAG loop.**

labs/common/lab5_agent_solution.txt.anchor: # ── Discover available tools from the MCP server ───────────
labs/common/lab5_agent_solution.txt.title: Discover tools and seed prompt
note: **Fetches the MCP server's tools at runtime and builds the system prompt and message list.**
- Local search_offices plus remote MCP tools are all in play

labs/common/lab5_agent_solution.txt.anchor: # Ask the LLM what to do next
labs/common/lab5_agent_solution.txt.title: Get the next action
note: **Asks the LLM what to do next and parses the Action, breaking if it can't.**

labs/common/lab5_agent_solution.txt.title: Finish and report
note: **When the LLM says 'done', prints the collected office info and weather.**

labs/common/lab5_agent_solution.txt.title: Route to local or MCP tool
note: **search_offices runs locally for RAG; every other action dispatches to the MCP server.**
- One loop, two tool sources — the heart of this lab

labs/common/lab5_agent_solution.txt.endAnchor: Args: <valid JSON arguments for the tool>
labs/common/lab5_agent_solution.txt.title: System prompt: local + MCP tools
note: **Advertises the local search_offices tool plus the runtime-discovered MCP tools.**
- search_offices must run first — it's the RAG retrieval step
- {mcp_tool_descriptions} is filled in at runtime

labs/common/lab5_agent_solution.txt.anchor: Thought: I need to search for office information about HQ
labs/common/lab5_agent_solution.txt.title: System prompt: worked example
note: **End-to-end example: search offices, geocode, weather, convert.**
- Shows the LLM the full multi-tool TAO sequence

labs/common/lab5_agent_solution.txt.anchor: Do NOT put arguments on the Action line. Arguments go ONLY in the Args line as JSON.
labs/common/lab5_agent_solution.txt.endAnchor: 7. Do NOT add extra text beyond the three lines
labs/common/lab5_agent_solution.txt.title: System prompt: guardrails
note: **Ordering and formatting rules that keep the agent reliable.**
- Always search first; args go only in the Args line; no invented data
